scenarios.avoid_when: Use OpenMM or MDAnalysis-specific skills for simulation engine or trajectory analysis after OpenFF has produced system/topology artifacts; use generic RDKit/datamol skills for chemistry tasks that do not involve OpenFF objects or SMIRNOFF force fields.
scenarios.best_for: Using or troubleshooting OpenFF Toolkit APIs for molecules, topology/PDB systems, SMIRNOFF .offxml force fields, OpenMM/Interchange handoff, and optional backend registry behavior.
scenarios.read_when: User asks about openff-toolkit, Open Force Field Toolkit, openff.toolkit, Molecule, Topology, ForceField, SMIRNOFF, OFFXML, toolkit_registry, RDKit/OpenEye/AmberTools/NAGL wrappers, OpenFF force fields, parameter handlers, label_molecules, create_interchange, create_openmm_system, PDB unique_molecules, conformers, partial charges, SMARTS/SMIRKS, or force-field assignment errors.
scenarios.role: OpenFF Toolkit skill for molecule processing, topology/system assembly, SMIRNOFF force-field loading/editing/application, and backend wrapper troubleshooting.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose openff-toolkit when the task involves OpenFF Toolkit objects, SMIRNOFF .offxml force fields, OpenFF force-field assignment, toolkit wrapper registry behavior, or preparing OpenFF topologies for Interchange/OpenMM. Choose a different chemistry/simulation skill when the request names another package and does not need OpenFF Toolkit APIs.
scenarios.title: Molecular ML, Drug Discovery, and Chemistry Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Cheminformatics, molecular mechanics force-field, SMIRNOFF, molecule/topology conversion, conformer, partial charge, PDB preparation, OpenMM/Interchange handoff, and optional chemistry backend tasks.
